selected_important_turn.text: Since I'll be foraging for water and spending more energy on that, how about I take 2 food and you get 1.  Bearing in mind that you're getting all of the stable water supply.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the deal-shaping proposal that actually divides the last contested resource (food). It trades on prior concessions (you gave up water) and effectively defines the final allocation. Changing this turn would directly change who gets what and the final payoff for both parties.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although fair and ultimately accepted, the proposal is framed as a near-final demand rather than an explicitly collaborative trade offer. It could be perceived as slightly self-favoring (2 vs 1) without a clear invitation for the other to suggest alternatives. That risks making the partner feel the split was imposed rather than jointly discovered and misses an opportunity to reinforce fairness and relationship goodwill.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Make the proposal more explicitly collaborative: briefly restate the tradeoffs you offered (water/firewood concessions), present the 2-1 split as a suggested trade rather than a firm claim, and invite the partner to comment or propose alternatives (e.g., suggest contingency if they prefer a different split or offer a small concession in another dimension).

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: slightly increase (by encouraging the partner to co-create any final tweaks that could improve utility for both)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (partner feels more involved/perceives the outcome as jointly agreed rather than imposed)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: neutral to slight increase (could speed acceptance by reducing any latent resistance)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (inviting input and acknowledging tradeoffs builds rapport and trust)

value2reason.Low: You're never out of potential firewood in a forest
value2reason.Medium: Water can be found in the wild and purified
value2reason.High: Food is very important and harder to forage for in the wild if you run out.

value2reason.Low: I was able to already get some firewood from the campground. I don't really need much more!
value2reason.Medium: Again, with the diabetes, I need to make sure I keep my blood sugar level stable with food!
value2reason.High: I have diabetes which tends to make me more thirsty.

dialogue.text: Hello! I'm so excited for this camping trip. What do you still need as your supplies?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think food, water, and firewood are all going to be fairly important.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree. I have diabetes, so water and food are super important to me. My diabetes makes me super thirsty and I have to make sure I keep my blood sugar under control with certain foods ☹️
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can definitely relate to that, I have family members with type 2 so I know how bad that can get
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes definitely! It is a pain for sure, but I love camping so I still want to go! I just have to make sure I have all the supplies I need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can do without extra water as long as I have food and firewood to purify water I find.  It might be better for you to have a reliable supply though.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That totally makes sense! I actually am good on firewood thankfully, I got some from a person selling it from their yard a little bit ago.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I would have been able to bring more with me by getting some from my parents, but my father decided he wanted to get rid of what he had left and so has been enjoying his fire pit a lot lately.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Oh I have always wanted a firepit! I totally understand. If you'd like you can have all 3 of the firewood supply stash to get you started.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: And I think it's entirely reasonable for you to have all 3 of the water as dehydration can be very bad very quickly, especially with diabetes.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds like a fair deal. Food wise, how would you like to split that up?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Since I'll be foraging for water and spending more energy on that, how about I take 2 food and you get 1.  Bearing in mind that you're getting all of the stable water supply.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'd like to propose a final split based on our prior trade: you take 2 Food, 1 Water, and 1 Firewood, while I take 1 Food, 2 Water, and 2 Firewood. This balances our concessions and prioritizes your needs. Would you like to adjust or suggest an alternative?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
